title: About John - John Healey MP
meta_description:
John is the Labour Member of Parliament for Rawmarsh and Conisbrough in South Yorkshire and is Secretary of State for Defence.
He has previously held the following positions in the Shadow Cabinet and Labour Government:

Shadow Secretary of State for Housing
Shadow Secretary of State for Health
Minister for Housing and Planning
Minister for Local Government
Minister for Floods Recovery
text_excerpt: About John - John Healey MP Skip to content Search for: Home Information About John Help & Advice Get In Touch News Campaigns Defence Pride in Place Search for: Home Information About John Help & Advice Get In Touch News Campaigns Defence Pride in Place Home Information About John Help & Advice Get In Touch News Campaigns Defence Pride in Place About John You are here: Home About John About John 2025-02-27T13:55:10+00:00 John is the Labour Member of Parliament for Rawmarsh and Conisbrough in South Yorkshire and is Secretary of State for Defence. He has previously held the following positions in the Shadow Cabinet and Labour Government: Shadow Secretary of State for Housing Shadow Secretary of State for Health Minister for Housing and Planning Minister for Local Government Minister for Floods Recovery (2007) Financial Secretary to the Treasury Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State for Adult Skills He has also been a member of the Education and Employment Select Committee and Parliamentary Private Secretary to the Chancellor of the Exchequer. John was elected MP for Wentworth in 1997. Boundaries changed at the 2010 general election and he became the MP for Wentworth and Dearne. Boundaries changed again at the 2024 general election and he became the MP for the newly created Rawmarsh and Conisbrough constituency. John was born in Wakefield and educated at Lady Lumley’s Comprehensive School, Pickering, and St Peter’s School in York and Christ’s College, Cambridge. He worked in the voluntary sector from 1984 to 1990 with the Royal National Institute for the Deaf, the Royal Association for Disability and Rehabilitation and MIND campaigning to improve rights and services for disabled people. From 1990 to 1994 John worked in communications for Issue Communications, a PR company, and the MSF Union. He was Campaigns Director for the TUC from 1994 to 1997. He has also been a part-time tutor at the Open University’s Business School. John is married with one son and lives in Rotherham.
